speaker: WARWICK
text: Trust me, my lord, all hitherto goes well.

speaker: WARWICK
text: The common people by numbers swarm to us.

speaker: WARWICK
text: But see where Somerset and Clarence comes.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Speak suddenly, my lords: are we all friends?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Fear not that, my lord.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Then, gentle Clarence, welcome unto Warwick,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And welcome, Somerset. I hold it cowardice

speaker: WARWICK
text: To rest mistrustful where a noble heart

speaker: WARWICK
text: Hath pawned an open hand in sign of love;

speaker: WARWICK
text: Else might I think that Clarence, Edward’s brother,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Were but a feignèd friend to our proceedings.

speaker: WARWICK
text: But welcome, sweet Clarence; my daughter shall be thine.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And now, what rests but, in night’s coverture

speaker: WARWICK
text: Thy brother being carelessly encamped,

speaker: WARWICK
text: His soldiers lurking in the town about,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And but attended by a simple guard,

speaker: WARWICK
text: We may surprise and take him at our pleasure?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Our scouts have found the adventure very easy;

speaker: WARWICK
text: That, as Ulysses and stout Diomed

speaker: WARWICK
text: With sleight and manhood stole to Rhesus’ tents

speaker: WARWICK
text: And brought from thence the Thracian fatal steeds,

speaker: WARWICK
text: So we, well covered with the night’s black mantle,

speaker: WARWICK
text: At unawares may beat down Edward’s guard

speaker: WARWICK
text: And seize himself. I say not “slaughter him,”

speaker: WARWICK
text: For I intend but only to surprise him.

speaker: WARWICK
text: You that will follow me to this attempt,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Applaud the name of Henry with your leader.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why then, let’s on our way in silent sort.

speaker: WARWICK
text: For Warwick and his friends, God and Saint George!
